# Elmer Simulation

*aka majordome_simulation.elmer*

```{python}
from majordome_utilities.documents import *
from majordome_simulation.elmer import *
```

## TimeStepAccumulator

```{python}
doc = DocumentedClass(TimeStepAccumulator)
doc.constructor()
doc.members()
```

In general the usage of the `TimeStepAccumulator` is as follows:

```{python}
acc = TimeStepAccumulator()
acc.add_block(end_time=1e-5, time_step=1e-6, save_interval=1e-5)
acc.add_block(end_time=1e-4, time_step=1e-5, save_interval=9e-5)
acc.add_block(end_time=1e-3, time_step=1e-4, save_interval=9e-4)

print(str(acc))
```

## ConstantTimeStepInterval

In some special cases, when using `TimeStepAccumulator` you might want to add time steps one by one, which can be done using the `append` method in combination with the `ConstantTimeStepInterval` described below.

```{python}
doc = DocumentedClass(ConstantTimeStepInterval)
doc.constructor()
doc.members()
```

## ElmerConvergenceData

```{python}
doc = DocumentedClass(ElmerConvergenceData)
doc.constructor()
doc.members()
doc.properties()
```

## ElmerTabularData

```{python}
doc = DocumentedClass(ElmerTabularData)
doc.constructor()
doc.properties()
```

## ElmerTabularMetadata

```{python}
doc = DocumentedClass(ElmerTabularMetadata)
doc.constructor()
doc.properties()
```
